title: Electromagnetic Clutch Circuit Check (Cont'd)

figures_detected.description: Section B diagram showing harness connector and brush holder connector with voltmeter (V) connected, brake pedal and selector shown, labels: Harness connector, Brush holder connector, terminals 1 and 2 on each side, label A and D

CONSULT monitor display showing: MONITOR NO FAIL, CAR SPEED SEN 0 km/h, ENGINE REV 0 rpm, CLUTCH CRRNT* 0.0A, CLUTCH CRRNT 0.2A, PULLEY RATIO 0.00, ACCEL SW OFF, THRTL OPEN SW OFF, AIR/C SIGNAL OFF, STOP LAMP SW OFF, with RECORD button
Section C diagram showing brush holder connector (terminals 1, 2) connected via ohmmeter to N-CVT C/U connector (terminals 1, 28), with DISCONNECT symbol

content_blocks.title: Electromagnetic Clutch Circuit Check - Section B (Continued from previous page)

nodes.text:
Check input signal
1. Remove brush holder connector as shown in figure and perform following procedures:
• Start engine and maintain at idle speed
• Pull parking brake lever and move selector to "D"
Check that voltage and current conform to following values:
Current: 0.1 to 0.2 A
Voltage: 0.2 to 1.8 V
Note: the CONSULT data monitor (actual clutch current) can be used to read the current value

Check harness continuity
1. Remove N-CVT control unit connector and brush holder connector
2. Check continuity between the N-CVT control unit harness connector terminals #1 and #28 and brush holder connector terminals #1 and #2
#1 → #1: 0Ω
#28 → #2: 0Ω
Repair or replace harness between N-CVT control unit and brush holder

model_notes: N-CVT (continuously variable transmission) electromagnetic clutch circuit. Applies to automatic transmission variant of K11 Micra.
